item.title: Jessica Monette: Root Me in the Soil, In the Wake and the Becoming (Project Room Exhibition)

copy.x: Jessica Monette's new exhibition at SCU's de Saisset Museum opens tomorrow—exploring family, memory, and what stays with us. Free to visit. https://events.scu.edu/de-saisset/event/351711-jessica-monette-root-me-in-the-soil-in-the-wake
copy.threads: Jessica Monette's "Root Me in the Soil, In the Wake and the Becoming" opens tomorrow at Santa Clara University's de Saisset Museum. It's an intimate look at familial memory, presence, and absence—the kinds of things that shape how we understand home and place. Free admission. https://events.scu.edu/de-saisset/event/351711-jessica-monette-root-me-in-the-soil-in-the-wake
copy.bluesky: Jessica Monette's new project room exhibition at SCU's de Saisset Museum opens tomorrow morning—exploring family, memory, and place in a way that stays with you. Free. https://events.scu.edu/de-saisset/event/351711-jessica-monette-root-me-in-the-soil-in-the-wake
copy.facebook: Jessica Monette's "Root Me in the Soil, In the Wake and the Becoming" opens tomorrow at the de Saisset Museum on SCU's campus. This project room exhibition is all about familial memory, presence, and what grounds us—definitely worth an hour if you're in Santa Clara. Free admission. https://events.scu.edu/de-saisset/event/351711-jessica-monette-root-me-in-the-soil-in-the-wake

item.title: Lecture by Zsolt Jozsef Simon — Design of Porcelain Objects at Art & Design Building

copy.x: Wednesday at SJSU: Zsolt József Simon talks porcelain design inspired by nature and ancient art. His plaster molding technique creates forms you've probably never seen. Free, 5pm at the Art & Design Building. https://events.sjsu.edu/event/art-design-lecture-by-zsolt-jozsef-simon
copy.threads: Wednesday evening at San Jose State — designer Zsolt József Simon is giving a lecture on porcelain objects. He works with ancient art and microscopic details from nature to push ceramic form into unexpected territory. His plaster molding technique is genuinely innovative. Free, 5pm at the Art & Design Building. https://events.sjsu.edu/event/art-design-lecture-by-zsolt-jozsef-simon
copy.bluesky: Wednesday at SJSU: Zsolt József Simon lectures on porcelain design—inspired by ancient art and nature's microscopic details. His plaster molding technique creates dynamic forms worth seeing. Free, 5pm, Art & Design Building. https://events.sjsu.edu/event/art-design-lecture-by-zsolt-jozsef-simon
copy.facebook: If you're into ceramics or design, Zsolt József Simon is speaking Wednesday at San Jose State. He designs porcelain objects inspired by ancient art and the microscopic beauty in nature—and uses an innovative plaster molding technique that really stands out. It's free and starts at 5pm at the Art & Design Building. https://events.sjsu.edu/event/art-design-lecture-by-zsolt-jozsef-simon

item.title: Neutrinos: From Pauli’s Desperate Remedy to Modern Day Precision Measurements

copy.x: Tomorrow at SCU: neutrino physicist Dr. Brian Mong from SLAC breaks down how these ghostly particles went from theoretical desperation move to cutting-edge science. Free, 4pm. 🧪 https://events.scu.edu/arts-and-sciences/event/451955-neutrinos-from-paulis-desperate-remedy-to-modern
copy.threads: Tomorrow afternoon at Santa Clara University, neutrino physicist Dr. Brian Mong (SLAC) is talking about how these weird, nearly-undetectable particles went from a "wait, this doesn't add up" moment in physics to modern precision measurements. Free, 4pm. 🧪 https://events.scu.edu/arts-and-sciences/event/451955-neutrinos-from-paulis-desperate-remedy-to-modern
copy.bluesky: Tomorrow at SCU: Dr. Brian Mong from SLAC on neutrinos—from Pauli's theoretical fix to modern precision measurements. How these ghost particles changed physics. Free, 4pm 🧪 https://events.scu.edu/arts-and-sciences/event/451955-neutrinos-from-paulis-desperate-remedy-to-modern
copy.facebook: Tomorrow afternoon at Santa Clara University, neutrino physicist Dr. Brian Mong from SLAC is diving into one of physics' wildest stories: how neutrinos went from Pauli's "we need this to work" moment to the precision measurements scientists use today. Free, 4pm. https://events.scu.edu/arts-and-sciences/event/451955-neutrinos-from-paulis-desperate-remedy-to-modern

item.title: Pelicans, Herons, and Egrets, Oh My!

Joan Sparks (wildlife photographer) is taking us on a deep dive into pelicans, herons & egrets—50 million years of wading bird evolution in one evening. Thursday at Los Altos library, 7pm, free. 🦅

https://sccl.bibliocommons.com/events/69af55b92866a5b488410573
copy.threads:
Wildlife photographer Joan Sparks is leading a talk about pelicans, herons, and egrets this Thursday at the Los Altos library. You'll learn about these elegant long-legged birds—whose ancestors show up in fossils dating back 50 million years. 7pm, completely free. 🦅

https://sccl.bibliocommons.com/events/69af55b92866a5b488410573
copy.bluesky:
Joan Sparks (wildlife photographer) dives into pelicans, herons & egrets Thursday night—50 million years of wading bird history in one evening. Los Altos library, 7pm, free. 🦅

item.title: Patient No More Exhibition at King Library

copy.x: Right now at SJSU King Library: Patient No More, about disability activists who occupied a federal building for 26 days in 1977 and won civil rights that still matter today. Free, opens at 8am. https://events.sjsu.edu/event/patient-no-more-exhibition
copy.threads: Right now at SJSU King Library: Patient No More exhibition. In 1977, disability activists occupied a federal building for 26 days and fundamentally changed U.S. civil rights.
